prep: Place in the transparent recycling bag
Plastic (PET).notes: PET bottles must be label-free and clean. Korea has strict sorting requirements.

prep: Sort by resin type if required by local municipality
Plastic.notes: Plastics (PE, PP, PS) go in transparent recycling bags. Some types may require separate sorting.

prep: Deposit in the glass recycling bin
Glass.notes: 빈용기보증금 (empty container deposit) applies to most glass bottles — return to point of purchase.

Aluminum.notes: Aluminum cans are highly valuable — always separate from other waste.

Metal.notes: Large metal items → community recycling center (재활용 센터).

prep: Tie into a bundle with string or place in paper recycling area
Cardboard.notes: Korea requires cardboard to be bundled separately, not in bags.

Paper.notes: Receipt paper (thermal) usually goes in general waste.

prep: Purchase designated 음식물 쓰레기 봉투 (food waste bag) from convenience store/supermarket
Organic.notes: VOLUME-BASED FEE SYSTEM: you must purchase official food waste bags (종량제 봉투) to dispose of food waste legally. Cost depends on bag size.

prep: Use designated e-waste collection boxes at apartment complexes or community centers
Large appliances: contact 한국전자제품자원순환공제조합 for pick-up
E-Waste.notes: Korea has a mandatory take-back program (생산자책임재활용제도 / EPR).

prep: Purchase official 종량제 봉투 (volume-rate bag) from convenience store or supermarket
All non-recyclable waste must go in this bag
Mixed Waste.notes: VOLUME-BASED FEE SYSTEM: disposing of waste in unmarked bags is illegal and subject to fines.

prep: Place in 의류 수거함 (clothing collection box) found in most apartment complexes
Textile.notes: Damaged textiles unsuitable for donation → general waste in 종량제 봉투.

prep: Place in official waste bag (종량제 봉투)
